title: Thelypteris multiseta
taxonomy: Thelypteris multiseta belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is a type of fern that is native to tropical regions of the world. It is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. Furthermore, it is also known as a member of the order Polypodiales and family Thelypteridaceae. Thelypteris multiseta is a type of vascular plant that reproduces via spores.

intro: Thelypteris multiseta, also known as the many-bracted maiden fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it a valuable remedy for promoting overall health and well-being. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris multiseta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
